steps.title: Match the singular subject
steps.content: The subject is The Proto-Atlantic-Congo language, which is singular. The phrase common ancestor of 1,453 African languages adds description but does not change the subject's number.

steps.title: Reject plural verbs
steps.content: A uses were, a plural or past-subjunctive form that does not fit this present statement. C uses the plural are. D uses have been, whose auxiliary does not agree with the singular subject. The answer is B.
